title: salix × aberrans
intro: Salix × aberrans, a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between two different species of willows, is a type of flowering plant that is native to Europe. The bark and leaves of Salix × aberrans contain salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The medicinal properties of Salix × aberrans have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, as well as to reduce pain and inflammation. Salix × aberrans can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
